name: AOSP SystemUI LongScreenshotActivity
platform: Android SystemUI / AOSP; ScrollCapture API (Android 12+, API 31+)
capture_driver: SystemUI drives ScrollCaptureClient, receives a ScrollCaptureResponse, then renders and exports a stitched long screenshot
compose_support: Partial; works when the underlying scroll container exposes ScrollCapture support
view_support: Scrollable app content, including supported web and view-based content
stitching_strategy: Tile-based vertical capture with preview and trimming
scroll_offset_trust: Uses ScrollCapture response bounds and render coordinates rather than pixel matching
duplicate_frame_handling: No explicit dedupe in the activity; capture is tile/session driven
seam_selection_policy: Fixed tile boundaries with user trim controls
confidence_fallback: Falls back to cached image load, cancels on capture failure, and closes the task if no screenshot is available
static_chrome_handling: Designed to trim top and bottom chrome such as status bars, headers, and sticky UI
dynamic_content_robustness: Best for mostly stable pages; live content may change while tiles are captured
content_type_fitness: Good for articles, long feeds, chats, and WebView pages
memory_strategy: Tile-based capture plus temp-file cache and bitmap export
stitching_speed: Asynchronous and page-size dependent; not documented [uncertain]
max_output_constraints: Limited by bitmap size, tile count, and temp-file or memory constraints
frame_interval: Driven by ScrollCapture requests; not fixed
output_format: PNG temp cache and exported image URI
oem_compatibility: Depends on device ScrollCapture support and SystemUI screenshot behavior
integration: Framework-private SystemUI activity wired into the screenshot flow
windowing_behavior: Captures within the target window bounds and handles rotation or orientation changes via preview transitions
permissions_required: SystemUI or framework-private access; no app runtime permission
